subject: [PATCH v1 01/13] fuse: fix missing forget requests in error paths

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the patch introduces a repeating pattern and suggested hiding it behind a helper function to validate expected attributes
reviews.raw_body:
This seems like a repeating pattern.
Would you consider hiding this behind a helper that validates we got
an expected set of attr:

        if (fuse_invalid_attr_ftype(&outarg.attr, mode & S_IFMT))

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to add a new helper function as requested by the reviewer, indicating that the patch will need further revision.
reviews.raw_body:
I will add this helper.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module where forget requests were not being sent to the server when it returned valid node IDs but invalid attributes, leading to potential server-side leaks. The patch modifies error paths in the fuse_lookup_name and fuse_create_open functions to send forget requests when necessary. This ensures that the server releases its reference on the file, preventing memory leaks. Although the real-world impact is minimal due to the specific conditions required for this bug to occur, sending forget requests is still essential for maintaining a healthy connection with the FUSE server.
